task_completion.description: Verify the finance_data.csv file is loaded into Power BI
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data/Fields pane on the right side of the Power BI interface shows the loaded table (likely named 'finance_data') with columns such as Date, Revenue, and Division.

task_completion.description: Verify a measure named 'Fiscal_YTD' is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data/Fields pane shows a calculator icon next to the name 'Fiscal_YTD'. The DAX formula bar may also be visible showing the calculation (e.g., using DATESYTD with a '03/31' parameter).

task_completion.description: Verify a Clustered Column Chart is used for visualization
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report canvas contains a Clustered Column Chart (vertical bars). In the Visualizations pane, the Clustered Column Chart icon should be highlighted/selected.
In the Visualizations pane for the chart, the X-axis field contains 'Month' (or Date hierarchy Month) and the Y-axis/Values field contains the 'Fiscal_YTD' measure.

task_completion.description: Verify the chart's X-axis is sorted chronologically from April to March
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the X-axis labels on the column chart. The leftmost column MUST be April (or Apr), followed by May, Jun, Jul, Aug, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec, Jan, Feb, and ending with March (or Mar) on the far right.

task_completion.description: Verify the report is saved as Fiscal_Report.pbix on the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Power BI application title bar displays 'Fiscal_Report' or 'Fiscal_Report.pbix', or a file named 'Fiscal_Report.pbix' is visible on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual Power BI Desktop application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or scripts generating the .pbix file.

integrity.description: Verify the chart and measures are genuinely created within Power BI
integrity.visual_evidence: The chart should be rendered natively in the Power BI canvas with standard UI elements (selection borders, tooltips, axis lines). The DAX formula bar should show actual code for the measure, not pasted text.

integrity.description: Verify the file was saved through the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog box should be visible in the screenshots targeting the Desktop directory, or the Power BI window title should update to reflect the new filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The source data contains 'Date', 'Revenue', and 'Division' columns. The required measure must be named 'Fiscal_YTD' and typically uses the DAX function DATESYTD with a year-end parameter of '03/31' or '3/31'. The most critical visual check is the Clustered Column Chart's X-axis: it MUST display the months in the exact fiscal year order: Apr, May, Jun, Jul, Aug, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec, Jan, Feb, Mar. The final output must be saved as 'Fiscal_Report.pbix' on the Desktop.
